preamble: The transformation of a voiceless velar fricative [x] into a voiceless uvular plosive [q] is a case of fortition. This strengthening of a fricative into a stop is less common but occurs in specific morphological contexts or loanword adaptations.

examples.note: Strengthening of [x] to [q] in certain loanword phonology environments where the source sound is perceived as requiring a more 'forceful' articulation.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
